initiatives.title: UDIT Generative AI Pilot Program
initiatives.description: UD Information Technologies launched a pilot inviting proposals to explore GenAI in academic, research, and administrative settings.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: CIS AI and ML research area
initiatives.description: UD Computer and Information Sciences describes AI/ML research across multi-agent systems, reinforcement learning, deep learning, health, biomedical, education, and geographic data.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

initiatives.title: AI/ML curriculum and laboratories

initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

initiatives.title: GenAI for career preparation
initiatives.description: UD's career center advises students to use GenAI for brainstorming, resumes, LinkedIn summaries, and interview practice while retaining human judgment.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.title: AI pilot proposal intake
initiatives.description: The UDIT pilot creates a proposal-based intake path for campus AI use cases that can shape future institutional adoption.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 institution moving AI into governed practice, research capacity, teaching, or workforce preparation.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Creates or relies on guardrails for privacy, academic integrity, or responsible use.

initiatives.title: DSI sponsored computational resources for AI and data-intensive research
initiatives.description: UD’s Data Science Institute describes access to DARWIN and DSI-owned Caviness nodes with NVIDIA T4 GPU hardware for interdisciplinary, GPU-enabled, data-intensive research.
initiatives.significance: This closes the compute-capacity gap at least partially with named clusters and GPU-enabled resources.

investment.description: Allocation requests for UD investigators are currently free; no total GPU count or facility cost disclosed.

collaborations.description: DSI and IT Research Computing shared campus resources.

tools_and_vendors.name: T4 GPUs
tools_and_vendors.role: GPU hardware in DSI-owned Caviness nodes

computing_infrastructure.summary: UD Data Science Institute notes access to DARWIN for GPU-enabled execution and owns Caviness nodes that include NVIDIA T4 GPU hardware; allocation requests for UD investigators are currently free, but the public page does not provide a total campus GPU count.

narrative_summary: University of Delaware shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 6 categories. The evidence emphasizes UDIT Generative AI Pilot Program, CIS AI and ML research area, and AI/ML curriculum and laboratories. Policy maturity is coded as permissive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.
